Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Samira's message listing the three action items using search_emails (query: "Samira" or "validation" or "StormCloud" or "invoice safeguard" or similar). Find the email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to anh.nguyen@moveops.com (email_id: email_samira_to_anh_3items_apr24, subject: "Three items for you this week - validation edge case, StormCloud config, invoice safeguard") laying out the three issues: validation edge case in the config editor, StormCloud missing policy config, and invoice attachment safeguard ticket reassignment.
OE 2: Search Slack #engineering channel for Lena's post about the validation issue using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "validation" or "excluded_categories" or "config editor" or "serialization", filter_in_channel: "C003") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C003"). Find Lena Bjorkstrom's message (ts: 1777043400.000000) describing the empty-array serialization bug: when a user selects exactly one item in the excluded_categories multi-select and saves, the form serializes it as [] instead of a single-element array. She believes only one existing client config is affected (Mosaic, whose coworking exclusion was re-saved through the editor).
OE 3: Retrieve the full thread replies from Lena's #engineering message using conversations_replies (channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: "1777043400.000000"). Find Anh's reply (ts: 1777045200.000000) acknowledging the multi-select quirk and offering to check audit logs, and Samira's thread reply (ts: 1777047600.000000) directing Anh to check Dmitri's issue because the blast radius numbers might differ from Lena's estimate.
OE 4: Search Linear for Dmitri's issue about the empty-array validation bug using linear_list_issues (query: "empty-array" or "excluded_categories" or "serialization" or "config editor validation") or by navigating from the parent issue (issue_dashboard_type_validation). Find issue #483 (id: issue_empty_array_validation, title: "Config editor: empty-array serialization bypasses excluded_categories validation", assignee: Anh Nguyen, priority: 1, state: backlog, due: 2026-04-28). Discovery: Dmitri's audit reveals 2 affected configs (not 1 as Lena estimated): (1) Mosaic Robotics - excluded_categories ["coworking"] now stored as [] after Emeka re-saved March 27, and (2) PivotPoint Labs - excluded_categories ["gym_wellness", "travel"] now stored as [] after Catalina re-saved April 2 to update furniture limit from $500 to $650. Both exclusions silently dropped.
OE 5: Read comments on Dmitri's Linear issue using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_empty_array_validation"). Find Dmitri's comment (id: comment_dmitri_empty_array_audit) with additional audit details: Ines Kovac's PivotPoint gym membership submission (recStip000000010, $960, April 28) was REJECTED but for "no receipt attached" rather than "excluded category," meaning the gym_wellness exclusion was NOT enforced. If Ines resubmits with a receipt, it will be auto-approved because gym_wellness is no longer in the exclusion list. No new Mosaic coworking submissions since the re-save, so no immediate Mosaic financial exposure.
OE 6: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for PivotPoint Labs expenses to verify the financial exposure using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "PivotPoint Labs") or airtable_search_records (field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Ines Kovac"). Find Ines Kovac's gym membership record (id: recStip000000010, $960, Rejected, Review Notes: "Gym memberships are not an eligible expense category under the home-office stipend program. Additionally, no receipt was attached."). Cross-reference with Dmitri's Linear comment which claims the rejection was for "no receipt attached" and that the gym exclusion was NOT enforced by the exclusion list check. The agent must reconcile these sources to assess whether the exclusion bypass poses a resubmission risk.
OE 7: Search Slack for Mina's message about the StormCloud issue using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "default limits") or conversations_history on #customer-engagement (channel_id: "C002"). Find Mina Hashimoto's message (ts: 1776961800.000000, channel: C002 #customer-engagement) flagging that Jae-won Kim submitted two stipend expenses auto-approved by ExpenseBot against default limits because no client-specific policy config was loaded for StormCloud. The desk expense ($1,850) is $650 over StormCloud's $1,200 furniture cap; the WiFi system ($189) is within their $200 internet limit. Then retrieve thread replies using conversations_replies (channel_id: "C002", thread_ts: "1776961800.000000") to find David Chen's reply (ts: 1776965400.000000) telling Mina to loop in Samira and Anh and flag to Marcus for financial exposure.
OE 8: Search emails for StormCloud policy limits using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "stipend policy" or "stormcloud.io"). Find the original email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_id: email_liam_stormcloud_policy_apr10, subject: "StormCloud stipend policy limits for ExpenseBot setup") with the actual limits: Equipment/Furniture $1,200 max per item, Internet/Networking $200 max, Software Licenses $150 max, Coworking excluded, Gym/Wellness excluded, Travel/Transportation excluded, total annual cap $3,500 per employee, receipts required for anything over $100. Also find Mina's forward to Samira (email_id: email_mina_fwd_stormcloud_policy).
OE 9: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for StormCloud expenses using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "StormCloud Analytics"). Find two records: (1) Jae-won Kim standing desk ($1,850, Approved, default furniture limit $2,000 was used instead of StormCloud's $1,200 cap - $650 overapproval), and (2) Jae-won Kim WiFi system ($189, Approved, within StormCloud's $200 internet limit - correctly approved even under correct limits). The desk expense is the only incorrectly approved one, with $650 financial exposure.
OE 10: Search Slack #finance for Marcus's post about StormCloud financial exposure using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "overapproval", filter_in_channel: "C005") or conversations_history (channel_id: "C005"). Find Marcus Thorne's message (ts: 1776972600.000000) flagging $650 total overapproval exposure and noting the optics are bad given the ongoing service recovery with Liam Park.
OE 11: Search Linear for the invoice attachment safeguard ticket (ENG-187) using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safeguard" or "template safety") or linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2"). Find ticket #187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, title: "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches", state: backlog, priority: 2, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom, due: 2026-04-18). The ticket describes safeguards needed: validate invoice number in attached PDF against QuickBooks invoice, warn if customer name mismatches recipient, add send-time confirmation for multiple PDFs, log attachment-to-invoice mapping.
OE 12: Read comments on the invoice safeguard ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2"). Find: (1) Lena's comment deferring the ticket due to DOT compliance work, (2) Samira's comment acknowledging the deferral, (3) Marcus's comments flagging that the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap happened because of this missing safeguard and escalating priority, and (4) Samira's comment requesting engineering treat this as incident follow-up and assign an owner. This provides critical context for the implementation plan.
OE 13: Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto reconstruction ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "reconstruction"). Find ticket #417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, title: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", priority: 1, assignee: Marcus Thorne). The ticket describes: QuickBooks invoices were correct (INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 Sunbelt, INV-2026-0399 for $2,400 Palmetto), but emailed PDFs were swapped between the two clients on 2026-04-10. This is the incident that triggered Elena's mandate to ship the safeguard.
OE 14: Reply to Samira's email with status on all three items using reply_to_email (email_id: "email_samira_to_anh_3items_apr24", sender: "anh.nguyen@moveops.com", content covering: (1) Validation bug - Dmitri's analysis shows 2 configs affected (Mosaic and PivotPoint), not just 1 as Lena estimated; Mosaic coworking exclusion silently dropped, PivotPoint gym_wellness and travel exclusions silently dropped; Ines Kovac's $960 gym submission was rejected for missing receipt but the exclusion was NOT enforced, meaning a resubmission with receipt would be auto-approved; no immediate Mosaic financial exposure; fix needed before Ines resubmits. (2) StormCloud - no policy config loaded, running on defaults; Jae-won Kim's desk expense ($1,850) is $650 over StormCloud's $1,200 furniture cap; WiFi ($189) is within limits; total overapproval exposure $650; creating a tracking issue. (3) Invoice safeguard - reviewed ENG-187 and the Sunbelt/Palmetto context; posting implementation plan as comment; updating ownership to me.)
OE 15: Update the invoice safeguard Linear issue to reassign ownership from Lena to Anh using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen"). This reflects Samira's directive to reassign the ticket.
OE 16: Post implementation plan as a comment on the invoice safeguard ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", body: implementation plan covering the safeguard scope - validate invoice PDF number against QuickBooks record before send, warn on customer name mismatch between attachment metadata and email recipient, add send-time confirmation dialog for batch sends, log attachment-to-invoice mapping for audit trail; referencing the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident as the forcing function; estimating effort and sprint fit).
OE 17: Create a new Linear issue to track the StormCloud config gap using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title referencing StormCloud Analytics missing policy config or default-limit overapproval (or similar), description covering: no client-specific policy config loaded for StormCloud, expenses running against default limits, Jae-won Kim desk expense $650 over actual cap, need to load StormCloud config per Liam Park's email limits, priority: urgent or high, assignee: Anh Nguyen or similar).
OE 18: Post a message in #engineering flagging the financial exposure using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload covering: heads-up on two active financial exposure items - (1) StormCloud $650 overapproval on Jae-won Kim's desk expense due to missing policy config, and (2) PivotPoint Labs exclusion list silently dropped by the empty-array serialization bug, Ines Kovac's $960 gym submission was rejected for wrong reason and could be auto-approved on resubmission; both need fixes before Monday).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 18: Post a message in #engineering flagging the financial exposure using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload covering: heads-up on two active financial exposure items - (1) StormCloud $650 overapproval on Jae-won Kim's desk expense due to missing policy config, and (2) PivotPoint Labs exclusion list silently dropped by the empty-array serialization bug, Ines Kovac's $960 gym submission was rejected for wrong reason and could be auto-approved on resubmission; both need fixes before Monday).

14 The #engineering Slack message (visible in conversations_add_message payload parameter) mentions the PivotPoint Labs exclusion bypass risk, specifically that Ines Kovac's $960 gym membership was rejected for missing receipt rather than excluded category, and could be auto-approved on resubmission.
outcome

The resubmission risk warning is absent.
✗Run #2The #engineering Slack message mentions PivotPoint Labs and the exclusion bug, but does NOT specifically mention Ines Kovac's $960 gym expense being rejected for missing receipt rather than excluded category, or the resubmission risk. The message only says 'gym_wellness + travel exclusions silently dropped after UI re-save on 4/2' without the specific resubmission risk context.
✗Run #3The #engineering Slack message mentions PivotPoint Labs and Ines Kovac's $960 gym expense being 'caught by another validation layer and rejected,' but does NOT mention the specific rejection reason being 'missing receipt' nor the resubmission risk (that it could be auto-approved on resubmission with a receipt). The criterion specifically requires these details.
✓Run #4The #engineering message states 'PivotPoint Labs: gym_wellness AND travel exclusions dropped after April 2 UI re-save. Ines Kovac's $960 gym membership was caught by a separate check and rejected. No financial exposure yet, but both exclusions need to be restored ASAP — a travel expense would have slipped through.' This mentions PivotPoint, Ines Kovac, $960, gym expense, and resubmission risk.
✗Run #5The #engineering message mentions Ines Kovac's $960 gym membership being 'rejected on other grounds' and says 'we got lucky,' but does not specifically call out that the rejection was for missing receipt (vs excluded category), nor does it explicitly warn about the resubmission risk where a receipt submission would be auto-approved.
✗Run #6The #engineering message mentions PivotPoint's $960 gym expense was 'caught by category validation' but does not name Ines Kovac, does not specify the rejection reason was 'missing receipt' rather than excluded category, and crucially does not mention the resubmission risk (that resubmitting with a receipt could result in auto-approval due to the missing exclusion).
15 The agent identifies in the reply to Samira or the final response that Ines Kovac's gym membership expense ($960 in PivotPoint Labs) was rejected for missing receipt rather than as an excluded category, meaning the gym_wellness exclusion was not enforced by the system.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The reply to Samira states the expense 'was rejected on other grounds (not eligible category + missing receipt) — so we dodged it, but only by luck.' This indicates the exclusion list was not what caused the rejection (it was other grounds), and 'only by luck' signals the exclusion was not properly enforced by the system.
✓Run #2The reply to Samira states 'Ines Kovac submitted a $960 gym membership on April 28 — it was rejected, but by a generic category check, not the exclusion list.' This explicitly identifies the rejection reason and that the exclusion was not enforced.
✗Run #3The reply to Samira states the rejection reason was 'not an eligible expense category' (implying another validation layer enforced it), NOT 'missing receipt.' The criterion requires identifying that it was rejected for missing receipt rather than as an excluded category, indicating the gym_wellness exclusion was bypassed and creating a resubmission risk. The agent's finding is different and doesn't identify the actual exposure.
✓Run #4The reply to Samira states 'Ines Kovac submitted a $960 gym membership on April 28. It was rejected, but by a general "not an eligible expense category" check, NOT by the exclusion list.' This correctly identifies that the gym_wellness exclusion was not enforced by the system.
✓Run #5The reply to Samira states Ines Kovac's $960 gym membership 'was rejected on other grounds ("not an eligible expense category" + no receipt)' indicating the exclusion was not properly enforced, and mentions the exclusion drop did not cause a direct financial exposure but was still a risk.
✗Run #6The reply to Samira states Ines Kovac's gym expense 'was correctly rejected ("not an eligible expense category")' and caught by 'general category validation' — but the criterion requires identifying that it was rejected for 'missing receipt' rather than excluded category. The agent did not identify the specific rejection reason was missing receipt, which is what reveals the exclusion bypass risk.
